notes: Same artifacts as submission 1/2 EXCEPT: (a) platt.json is identity (a=1, b=0, same as sub 2), and (b) model.py has the mean-residual subject offset DISABLED in predict() — the labeled argument is ignored. Diagnostic: tests whether the K=5 mean-residual offset (added in our rewrite to replace baseline's Newton-Raphson theta update) is helping or hurting versus pure cold MLP predictions. Reference: sub 2 was raw sigmoid + offset and scored NLL=-0.93, AUC=0.63. If sub 3 scores meaningfully better than -0.93, the offset is hurting; if it scores meaningfully worse, the offset is helping; if it's a wash, the offset is neutral.
